touches.ai_summary: Cold outreach to Sebastian Carter at LunarMedia introducing our call intelligence platform for rep productivity. Highlighted automation capabilities and how teams reduce manual note-taking. Sebastian responded expressing interest in learning more.
next_steps: Schedule qualification call to discuss current RevOps workflow

touches.ai_summary: Initial qualification call with Sebastian. Discussed LunarMedia's current state: 24 sales reps manually logging calls, no structured call intelligence. Sebastian mentioned they're evaluating Fathom as well. Confirmed budget authority sits with RevOps but final sign-off requires VP Sales approval.

questions_asked.question: What's driving the search for call intelligence now?
questions_asked.answer: Our reps spend 30+ minutes per day on call notes and CRM updates. We need to free up selling time.

questions_asked.question: How does your automation compare to Fathom?
questions_asked.answer: We offer unlimited automation across all plans while Fathom caps automations on lower tiers. I'll send a comparison doc.

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up qualification call diving into technical requirements. Sebastian walked through their HubSpot setup and integration needs. Discussed seat count (24 reps) and use case focused on automated CRM sync and meeting summaries. Sebastian confirmed timeline: wants solution live by end of quarter.

questions_asked.question: Can you sync summaries directly into HubSpot deal records?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we have native HubSpot integration with customizable field mapping.

questions_asked.question: Who else should be involved in evaluating this?
questions_asked.answer: Zoey Osei on my team will be the primary user for setting up automations. VP Sales will need to approve the budget.

questions_asked.question: What's the typical implementation timeline?
questions_asked.answer: Most customers are live within 5-7 business days after contract signature.

next_steps: Loop in Zoey for technical walkthrough

touches.ai_summary: Discovery call with Sebastian to finalize requirements. Confirmed unlimited_automation tier is required for their workflow automations. Discussed ROI: saving ~10 hours/week per rep. Sebastian asked about discount options for annual commit. Noted VP Sales hasn't been brought into conversations yet.

questions_asked.question: What kind of pricing flexibility do you have for annual deals?
questions_asked.answer: We can work with you on annual agreements. Let me put together a proposal based on 24 seats.

questions_asked.question: When can we get time with your VP Sales to align on priorities?
questions_asked.answer: Let me check their calendar. They're pretty slammed but I can brief them on what we've discussed.

objections: Annual pricing feels high compared to Fathom's quoted rate
next_steps: Send formal proposal with pricing options

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email sent with demo recording and trial access for Zoey to test automation workflows. Reiterated value prop around unlimited automations vs Fathom's limitations. Sebastian confirmed reviewing internally with finance.

questions_asked.question: Can you send over a couple customer case studies in eCommerce?
questions_asked.answer: Attached two case studies from similar-sized eCommerce companies seeing 40% reduction in CRM update time.

next_steps: Sebastian to share demo with VP Sales

touches.ai_summary: Proposal review call with Sebastian. Walked through pricing: $25,440 list for 24 seats unlimited_automation tier. Sebastian pushed back on price, referenced Fathom quote coming in lower. Discussed discount options to close this quarter. Agreed to 23% discount bringing ACV to $19,500. VP Sales still not on call.

questions_asked.question: What's your best price if we sign by end of month?
questions_asked.answer: I can get approval for 23% off annual list if you can commit this quarter, bringing it to $19,500.

questions_asked.question: Can we schedule time with your VP to ensure alignment before final decision?
questions_asked.answer: I'll try to set something up but they've delegated this to me to drive. Let's get the exec meeting on the books.

objections: Price is still higher than Fathom's competitive offer

touches.ai_summary: Scheduled exec meeting was rescheduled last minute due to VP Sales conflict. Brief call with Sebastian to confirm revised pricing proposal received and under legal review. Sebastian mentioned procurement is asking questions about data security and SOC 2 compliance.

questions_asked.question: Can you send our security team your SOC 2 report?
questions_asked.answer: Absolutely, I'll send that over today along with our security documentation.

next_steps: Reschedule VP Sales meeting for next week

touches.ai_summary: Negotiation call rescheduled once, finally connected with Sebastian. Legal review raised questions on data retention and MSA terms. Sebastian asked if we can go lower on price given delays and Fathom's aggressive pricing. Discussed payment terms: they want quarterly payments vs annual upfront. No VP Sales participation yet.

questions_asked.question: Can we do quarterly billing instead of annual upfront?
questions_asked.answer: Our discounted pricing requires annual payment. I can check if we can split into two installments.

questions_asked.question: What's the holdup on getting VP Sales engaged? Want to make sure we're aligned on value.
questions_asked.answer: They trust my evaluation. We're just working through procurement and legal now.

objections: Annual upfront payment is difficult for our cash flow this quarter
Still concerned we're overpaying vs alternatives

touches.ai_summary: Extended exec meeting finally secured with VP Sales, Sebastian, and procurement. Walked through ROI model, product demo highlights, and differentiation vs Fathom on automation capabilities. VP Sales expressed support but raised concerns about implementation timeline and team adoption. Discussed training plan and success metrics. Agreed to final terms: $19,500 ACV, 24 seats, split payment (60% upfront, 40% in 90 days).

questions_asked.question: What does onboarding look like and how do we ensure rep adoption?
questions_asked.answer: We provide dedicated onboarding with your team, plus train-the-trainer sessions for RevOps to drive adoption.

questions_asked.question: If this doesn't deliver the time savings promised, what's our out?
questions_asked.answer: We have a 30-day satisfaction guarantee. If you're not seeing value, we'll work with you or provide a refund.

questions_asked.question: Can we move forward with contracting this week?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, pending final legal approval on the MSA redlines. Should have signatures by end of week.

objections: Need to see faster time-to-value given the investment
